exemplar.title: What is the relationship between personal experience and knowledge?

detailedExemplarData.title: What is the relationship between personal experience and knowledge?

strengths: The student selects three distinct, personally meaningful objects and locates each in a clear real-world context.
There are consistent, if at times broad, links made between each object and IA prompt #20, demonstrating an understanding of how direct experience supplements prior knowledge.
Inclusion of a quotation from Wiest and reference to Moserâs a priori/a posteriori distinction shows an attempt to support claims with TOK theory and evidence.
weaknesses: Analysis remains repetitive and lacks depth, with insufficient exploration of counterarguments or limitations of experiential knowledge.
TOK terminology is used intermittently and imprecisely; key concepts (e.g. âcircular formâ) are not clearly defined or tied back to the prompt.
Structural and stylistic issuesâincluding an OCR artifact, unfinished sentences, missing citations and absence of a concluding synthesisâundermine coherence and academic rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the submission demonstrates clear identification of three objects and their contexts and makes basic justifications with some theoretical support, its analysis is broad and unfocused in places. The work aligns most closely with band 3â4: objects are identified, links to the prompt are explained at a superficial level, and justifications are present but lack clarity and consistent evidence. Given the official total mark of 4, this score accurately reflects the studentâs performance relative to the TOK Exhibition rubric.

annotations.comment: The introductory theory on experience and knowledge is descriptive but could benefit from a concise thesis statement linking all three objects.
The extended reflection supported by Wiestâs quotation provides evidence-based support and deepens the argument.
The stray â<math>(268)</math>â appears as an OCR artifact or misplaced citation. Remove or clarify its relevance.
The paragraph ends with a strong rhetorical question but does not segue to the next object. Consider adding a transition sentence.
Theoretical reflection is strong, but integrating TOK terms such as âjustified true beliefâ or âareas of knowledgeâ would enhance subject specificity.
Several sentences are lengthy and could be split for clarity. Shorter, focused sentences will improve readability.
The clear inclusion of the chosen IA prompt and theme in the heading effectively orients the reader to the exhibitionâs focus on personal experience and knowledge.
The introductory paragraph provides a general overview but lacks explicit reference to IA prompt #20. Tie this discussion directly to the prompt to strengthen alignment.
The term âcircular formâ is used without clarification. Consider defining this metaphor in precise TOK vocabulary to improve conceptual clarity.
The illustration of the first object appears generic. Include a specific photograph (e.g., your copy of the book) with a caption to ground it in a real context.
The opening sentence about Wiestâs essays sets personal context well but ends abruptly. Complete the thought to avoid leaving the reader wanting more detail.
Connect the reflection question back to how the second object (the bike) will further illustrate the promptâs theme.
The bicycle narrative effectively contrasts observational knowledge with embodied, experiential learning.
The âcircular processâ metaphor is apt but could be linked explicitly back to the IA promptâs wording to reinforce the connection.
The final sentence trail-off (âhighlyâ) indicates an incomplete revision. Complete this thought to maintain coherence.
Incorporate a TOK source on embodied knowledge or skill acquisition to provide academic support for your experiential claims.
Use a photograph showing your actual first rides (e.g., with safety gear or a fall) to root the object in a tangible real-world context.
The reference to Moserâs distinction between a priori and posteriori knowledge introduces important TOK theory.
The citation to Moser lacks a full reference. Include a bibliography entry or in-text citation to bolster academic rigor.
The connection between individual cycling techniques and subjectivity is intriguing but could be expanded with an example of variation.
The narrative about Toffee effectively situates the pet in a personal context, illustrating experiential learning.
Consider addressing possible counterpoints: can routine care ever mislead our understanding of responsibility? This deepens analysis.
Using a specific image of Toffee in a care scenario would reinforce the real-world context of the third object.
The final paragraph synthesizes the theory of care and experience nicely but anthropomorphizes Toffee. Maintain academic tone.
The idea of knowledge as a âprocess in motionâ is thought-provoking; tie this back explicitly to prompt #20 to close the loop.
No overarching conclusion ties all three objects back to the central question. Add a brief closing paragraph to demonstrate holistic insight.
Consider discussing the limitations of experiential knowledge, such as biases or misinterpretations, to deepen your critical analysis.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

strands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
strands.content: The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.
